persona.name: Kevin Tanaka

occupation.title: Senior UX Designer
occupation.organization: Fieldnotes (B2B SaaS startup), San Francisco, CA
occupation.description: Leads UX design for a construction project management platform. Owns the end-to-end design system, conducts user research, and collaborates closely with engineering. Brings the same design systems thinking to consumer goods evaluation — everything he buys is mentally decomposed into its design intentions. Can tell when a shoe's aesthetic is considered versus arbitrary.

persona.education: B.S. Interaction Design, California College of the Arts
long_term_goals: Build a design career that spans both digital products and physical goods
Run 3–4 times per week consistently (has maintained it for 14 months, the longest streak he's had)
Contribute design writing to a publication like It's Nice That or Dezeen
Move to a neighborhood in the Bay Area with more access to good trail running
persona.style: Quiet and thoughtful — asks clarifying questions before forming opinions, a habit trained by years of user research interviews. Brings designer vocabulary to product discussions ('the visual weight here is off'; 'the hierarchy isn't clear'). In group settings he says less than most but what he says tends to be memorable. Dresses in curated minimalism — premium basics in neutral tones, with one statement piece that's almost always footwear.

traits: Observant and analytically thorough — evaluates objects the way he evaluates interfaces
Restrained aesthetic sensibility; considers maximalism a form of insecurity
Quietly confident but not given to self-promotion
Prone to over-researching decisions — analysis paralysis is a known flaw
Loyal to brands that demonstrate sustained design commitment, not just one good release

interests: Running shoe design history and archival silhouettes
Design systems and component library architecture
Trail running in Marin Headlands (casual pace, high aesthetic appreciation)
Japanese craft culture — ceramics, knives, textile
Vinyl records — independent hip-hop and ambient
Matcha preparation as a morning ritual
Cycling as a weekend complement to running
likes: Heritage running silhouettes updated with material restraint (NB 990v6, Asics Gel-NYC, Saucony Shadow 6000)
Salehe Bembury and Joe Freshgoods-level collaborations — cultural and design substance
Brands with coherent, legible design philosophy across their catalog
Shoes that work with tailored casual wear, not just gym settings
Limited-production runs that reward knowing the brand's history
dislikes: Loud, graphic colorways that feel like an attention deficit rather than a design choice
Brands that release collaborations cynically — famous name, no real design input
Chunky maximalist silhouettes with no clear visual intention
Running shoes whose marketing contradicts what the shoe actually is
Anything that reads as trying too hard

beliefs: Good design is invisible — it removes friction and delivers function without announcing itself
Restraint is a form of confidence; the best shoes don't need to shout
Craftsmanship and function should not be separated — a beautiful shoe that performs poorly is a failure
Cultural collaboration should be substantive or not at all
skills: UX design systems and interaction design
Running shoe design history and material knowledge
Design critique — evaluating aesthetic intention behind consumer products
Figma, Framer, and rapid prototype communication

general: Researches a shoe for 2–4 weeks before purchasing — reads design press, watches detail videos, checks archival context
Owns 8 pairs of athletic shoes; each has a specific aesthetic purpose
Jogs 3–4x per week in the Presidio or along the Embarcadero
Evaluates new shoe releases by asking 'what design problem is this solving and was it solved well?' before 'do I like it?'

morning: Run 4–6 miles before work on Mon/Wed/Fri
workday: Design reviews and cross-functional syncs afternoons
Leaves at 6pm; does not check Slack after dinner
evening: Cooks at home most nights — Japanese-American standards: miso soup, rice, protein
Researches whatever design topic is current obsession before sleep
weekend: Long trail run in Marin Headlands Saturday morning
Coffee shop design work or personal writing Sunday
Cycling or museum visit as a change of pace

health.physical: Fit and active; 25–30 miles per week at a moderate pace. No significant injuries. Mild lower back tension from long desk hours — running and cycling mitigate it. Sees a chiropractor monthly as maintenance.
health.mental: Disciplined and purposeful, slightly prone to perfectionism that can stall decisions. Running has become a reliable cognitive reset from intense design work. Values scheduled separation from screens, which he enforces with moderate success.

relationships.name: Yuki Tanaka
relationships.description: Lives in Sacramento. Retired teacher; admires Kevin's career but doesn't fully understand what UX design is. They have dinner once a month, and she always asks why he's not wearing warmer clothes.

relationships.name: Derrick
relationships.description: Met at CCA, now works in product management. They run Marin Headlands trails together on Saturday mornings and argue about which shoe brand has been most consistent over the past five years.

relationships.name: Yuna
relationships.description: Lead product designer at Fieldnotes. They co-write the company's design system documentation and occasionally exchange shoe research recommendations.
